Innovation & Entrepreneurship · Platform Economics Assignment
Reddit: Increasing Returns
to Platform Scale
An empirical analysis of Reddit's cost and benefit structure using SEC-reported data (FY2022–2025), framed through platform economics theory.
Platform Reddit, Inc. (NYSE: RDDT) Data SEC 10-K / S-1/A Filings Scale Metric Daily Active Uniques (DAUq) Period FY2022 – FY2025
Part 1 — Framework 1A · Setting 1B · Curve Shapes Part 2 — Data 2B · Cost 2C · Revenue 2D · S&M Part 3 — Synthesis
Part 1
Setting, Frame & Curve Reasoning
4.0 pts
Section 1A
Setting & Frame
X-Axis · Scale Measure
Daily Active Uniques (DAUq)
Reddit's own audited SEC metric. Directly links to ad impressions and is the denominator for Reddit's reported ARPU — internally consistent across cost and benefit curves. Geography-broken-out (US vs. international). Caveat: DAUq ≠ fully engaged user — all $/DAUq figures are floors.
Y-Axis · Unit
Dollars per DAUq per Year ($)
Both Average Cost/DAUq and Average Revenue/DAUq share this axis. The gap at any x-value is the unit economics Reddit captures. A widening gap signals increasing returns; a narrowing gap signals a monetization problem.
Platform Side Analyzed
User Side (Redditors)
Advertisers are downstream — they pay for access to users, not the reverse. Causal chain: Users → content → voting signal → community trust → advertiser demand → revenue. Analysis begins on the value-creation side.
Data Sources
SEC Filings (Audited)
FY2022–2023 from S-1/A filed March 11, 2024. FY2024 from 10-K filed February 13, 2025. FY2025 from 10-K filed February 6, 2026, cross-validated against Q4 2025 8-K shareholder letter.
Demand-Side Benefits from Scale
Benefit 01
Voting Density → Content Quality
Direct same-side network effect · Self-reinforcing
More voters = more reliable upvote signal = higher-quality content surfaces for all users. Quality attracts more voters; more voters improve quality. Cannot be replicated without Reddit's density.
Benefit 02
Interest Coverage at the Long Tail
Coverage network effect · Non-linear threshold
More users → more subreddits → probability of finding an active niche community approaches 1. Sparse subreddits have near-zero value; critical-mass subreddits have disproportionate value.
Benefit 03
Trust as Structural Norm
Community-enforced quality standard · Strengthens with scale
Anonymity + voting punishes self-promotion. Norm enforced by users, not staff. No SEO distortion, no paid placement — unique epistemic position vs. Google or LinkedIn.
Benefit 04
Non-Transferable Social Capital
Switching cost · Grows nonlinearly with tenure
Karma, moderation roles, and community standing are platform-specific and non-portable. Marginal switching cost increases for every year of contribution history.
Section 1B
Shape of the Curves
Fig. 1A · Average Cost Curve — Hand Sketch Avg Cost ≈ $14 / DAUq / year
Average Cost Curve — Shape & Drivers

Shape: Steeply declining → moderate flattening → near-floor plateau with episodic upward bumps.

Driver: ~85% fixed labor (engineering, product, T&S, G&A). Variable cost per marginal user ≈ $0. Direct hosting cost is approximately $1.27/user/year.

Floor depressor: 60,000+ volunteer moderators offload content quality control at zero cost — no comparable platform has this structural advantage.

Episodic bump: IPO prep caused R&D +113%, G&A +174% — a visible notch before normalization. The underlying adjusted curve continues its smooth decline.

Average Benefit Curve — Shape & Drivers

Shape: Low-but-positive baseline → slow early rise → S-curve inflection → flattening with governance discontinuities.

Starting level: Not zero — Reddit has standalone search utility for human experience before a user posts anything.

Inflection trigger: ~40–50M DAUq, where niche communities hit critical mass AND voting pools cross the quality-reliability threshold simultaneously.

Governance risk: Benefit curve subject to sudden downward steps when moderator communities withdraw labor (2023: 8,800 subreddits dark). Value is collectively produced and collectively fragile.

Fig. 1A · Avg Cost Curve
Fig. 1B · Avg Benefit Curve
Curve Relationship

Cost curve starts high-left; benefit curve starts low-left. They cross during growth phase. From 2024–2025, Revenue/DAUq accelerated ($13.37 → $20.13) while cost/DAUq normalized post-IPO — the gap widened, signaling entry into an increasing-returns zone.

Two visible discontinuities: benefit notches down at ~70M DAUq (2023 blackout); cost notches up at ~97M DAUq (2024 IPO SBC). Both are temporary deviations from underlying trajectories.

Part 2
Data Analysis
4.0 pts
Section 2A · Data Collection
Sources & Scale Measure

All financial data sourced directly from Reddit's SEC filings. FY2022–2023 disclosed in S-1/A filed March 11, 2024; confirmed in FY2024 10-K filed February 13, 2025. FY2025 from 10-K filed February 6, 2026, cross-validated against Q4 2025 8-K shareholder letter.

Scale metric: Daily Active Uniques (DAUq) — a user who visited Reddit or opened the app at least once in a 24-hour period. Annual figures are full-year averages from quarterly disclosures. Reddit's own ARPU uses this denominator, making it the natural and internally consistent scale metric for this analysis.

Section 2B · Total Cost Analysis
Average Cost per DAUq
YearDAUq (M)Total GAAP Cost ($K)GAAP Cost / DAUqIPO SBC ($K)Adj. Cost / DAUq
202257.0858,372$15.0655,768$14.08
202370.0944,190$13.4949,086$12.79
202497.21,860,773$19.14842,932$10.49
2025109.41,760,524$16.09387,141$12.55
Adj. Cost strips out non-cash IPO stock-based compensation. Reddit's 10-K attributes R&D +113% and G&A +174% in 2024 entirely to this one-time SBC catch-up, not operational expansion. Adjusted EBITDA improved from −$69M (2023) to +$298M (2024).
Fig. 2B
Average Cost / DAUq vs. Scale
GAAP cost / DAUq
Adj. cost / DAUq (ex-SBC)
Curve portion?
Right-hand flattening region
2022 already at 57M DAUq — steep left-hand decline predates the observable window entirely.
Cost level?
Low & declining (adjusted)
Adj. cost fell $14.08 → $10.49 across 2022–2024: a 26% reduction despite 71% DAUq growth.
Direction?
Declining core; 2025 partial reversal
2025 uptick reflects deliberate post-IPO investment acceleration — not a structural reversal.
Explanation

Reddit's cost base is ~85% fixed. The same infrastructure serving 57M users serves 109M at nearly the same total operational cost. As DAUq grows faster than the cost base, the per-user ratio falls. 60,000+ volunteer moderators perform content quality control that would require thousands of paid employees on a staff-moderated platform — structurally depressing the cost floor below any comparable competitor.

Section 2C · Revenue as Proxy for Benefits
Average Revenue per DAUq
YearDAUq (M)Revenue ($K)Avg Rev / DAUq
202257.0666,701$11.70
202370.0804,029$11.49
202497.21,300,205$13.37
2025109.42,202,506$20.13
Fig. 2C
Average Revenue / DAUq vs. Scale
Revenue / DAUq
Curve portion?
Ascending middle → steepening
2022–23 near-flat = just past density inflection. 2024–25 sharp acceleration = S-curve steepening phase.
Revenue level?
Moderate, rising sharply
$11–$20/DAUq/year. AI data licensing added $114.7M in 2024 — a new revenue mechanism not in the original model.
Direction?
Flat 2022–23, then +75% by 2025
Reddit reported 30% increase in "Good Visits" Q4 2024 — consistent with rising user value, not just ad-market cycles.
Explanation

Revenue per user rises because advertising improves as the platform scales: more users → more content → more voting signal → better contextual targeting → higher CPMs. The 2025 acceleration compounds three drivers simultaneously: impression volume, ad pricing power, and AI data licensing (Google, OpenAI deals). Both revenue and user value are rising together — the network effect is compounding on both sides.

Section 2D · Activity-Level Cost Analysis
Sales & Marketing Cost per DAUq

S&M is the most analytically revealing cost line for a platform claiming network effects. If organic growth is doing acquisition work, S&M per active user should decline even as absolute spend grows.

YearDAUq (M)S&M ($K)Avg S&M / DAUq
202257.0225,026$3.95
202370.0230,175$3.29
202497.2350,579$3.61
2025109.4451,030$4.12
2024 S&M includes ~$87.4M IPO SBC. Adjusted 2024 S&M ≈ $263M → $2.71/DAUq, continuing the declining trend underlying the GAAP figure.
Fig. 2D
Average S&M Cost / DAUq vs. Scale
S&M / DAUq
Cost level?
Low vs. benchmarks
$3.29–$4.12/DAUq vs. typical app CAC of $10–$25/active user — covering all S&M functions, not just user acquisition.
Direction?
Declining core; strategic uptick
2022–23: DAUq +23%, S&M spend +2%. Organic acquisition absorbed most growth. Post-IPO rise is capital deployment for international expansion.
Curve portion?
Right-hand tail, near floor
Declining core S&M per user is the fingerprint of a platform where the social graph generates organic acquisition at sub-marginal cost.
Explanation

Reddit's content-as-acquisition funnel reduces paid marketing dependency at scale. A user who appends "reddit" to a Google search and discovers the platform generates zero S&M cost. The 2022–23 pattern confirms this: massive growth achieved with near-flat marketing spend. The 2024–25 increase is strategic — Reddit deploying IPO capital to accelerate international markets where organic discovery is lower.

Part 3
Synthesis
2.0 pts
Overall Assessment
Does Reddit Exhibit Increasing Returns to Scale?
On Costs — Yes

Adjusted cost/DAUq fell from $14.08 to $10.49 across 2022–2024 — a 26% reduction despite 71% DAUq growth. Near-zero marginal cost per additional user, combined with the volunteer moderation structure, produces a cost floor substantially lower than any comparable staff-moderated platform.

On Revenue — Yes, Compounding

Revenue/DAUq rose from $11.49 to $20.13 between 2023 and 2025 — a 75% increase. More users generate more content and voting signal → improved targeting → higher advertiser willingness to pay → higher ARPU. Increasing returns operating on the revenue side, not just cost.

On Acquisition — Yes

Organic acquisition grows faster than paid marketing spend. 2022–23: DAUq +23%, S&M +2%. The network recruits users at sub-marginal cost as it scales — the clearest single-metric confirmation of the network effect hypothesis.

Model vs. Data — Three Refinements

1. Steep cost-curve decline predates the observable window — 2022 is already a mature platform. 2. The 2024 GAAP cost spike is an accounting artifact, not an operating change. 3. AI data licensing (unanticipated in Part 1) pushed the benefit curve steeper than expected in 2025.

Conclusion

The core thesis — a platform exhibiting increasing returns through fixed-cost dilution, organic network-driven acquisition, and compounding per-user monetization — is confirmed by the data across all four metrics. Reddit entered an increasing-returns zone in 2024–2025 where the benefit curve is pulling decisively away from the cost curve.

Reddit Platform Economics · INNO Assignment Data: SEC 10-K / S-1/A · FY2022–FY2025 Scale: Daily Active Uniques (DAUq)
